actionUnits.condition: Validate mapping: Sphere has two KeyDetector MonoBehaviours matching the provided script (guid 4f1f41ed7c4cfc94d930897342a16527). MonoBehaviour id 33018426 has displayTag=Display, keypadTag=Keypad, keypadButtonTag=KeypadButton; MonoBehaviour id 33018431 has displayTag=Display2, keypadTag=Keypad2, keypadButtonTag=KeypadButton2. SphereCollider(id 33018427) isTrigger=0; Rigidbody present. This step: enter ButtonBack (fileID 951466313, BoxCollider isTrigger=1, KeyFeedback MonoBehaviour id 951466316) while display is empty to cover Back when no characters (no removal, sets KeyFeedback.keyHit=true).

Press Button(1) (fileID 195114625, tag KeypadButton2, BoxCollider isTrigger=1, KeyFeedback id 195114628). Expected: KeyDetector(33018431) OnTriggerEnter appends '1' to Display2 (max 4 chars), sets KeyFeedback.keyHit=true to play sound/flash.
Press Button(2) (fileID 1362274088, KeyFeedback id 1362274091). Expected: append '2' (now '12'), KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Press Button(3) (fileID 294570348, KeyFeedback id 294570351). Expected: append '3' (now '123'), KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Press Button(4) (fileID 691591083, KeyFeedback id 691591086). Expected: append '4' (now '1234' at max length), KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Overflow guard: press Button(5) (fileID 851985417, KeyFeedback id 851985420). Expected: no change to display because max 4 chars reached; KeyFeedback.keyHit=true still toggles feedback.
Press ButtonBack (fileID 951466313, KeyFeedback id 951466316). Expected: remove last character ('1234' -> '123'), KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Press Button(6) (fileID 2087223067, KeyFeedback id 2087223070). Expected: append '6' ('123' -> '1236'), at max length again; KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Attempt another overflow: press Button(7) (fileID 2122760608, KeyFeedback id 2122760611). Expected: still 4-char cap (no append), feedback plays via KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.

Press ButtoConfirm (fileID 1619357597, KeyFeedback id 1619357600). Expected: since display length > 0, KeyDetector(33018431) calls KeyPadControll.CheckIfCorrect(int). Display2 text becomes 'Correct' or 'Retry'; KeyFeedback feedback occurs.

Non-numeric reset path: press Button(8) (fileID 1249007308, KeyFeedback id 1249007311). If prior Confirm showed non-numeric text (e.g., 'Retry'), KeyDetector clears display then appends '8'. KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Press Button(9) (fileID 571652021, KeyFeedback id 571652024). Expected: append '9' (now '89' if cleared), KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Press Button(0) (fileID 354067704, KeyFeedback id 354067707). Expected: append '0' (e.g., '890'), KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.

Press ButtoConfirm again (fileID 1619357597). Expected: CheckIfCorrect invoked with current numeric display; Display2 updates to 'Correct' or 'Retry'; KeyFeedback feedback occurs. This concludes coverage for: number append, max length cap, backspace removal, non-numeric reset, and confirm validation.

Validation setup: Sphere has two KeyDetector MonoBehaviours that match the provided script (guid 4f1f41ed7c4cfc94d930897342a16527). MonoBehaviour id 28697939 is configured for Display2/Keypad2/KeypadButton2; MonoBehaviour id 28697942 is configured for Display/Keypad/KeypadButton. SphereCollider(id 28697943) isTrigger=0; Rigidbody present. The following triggers target KeypadButton2-tagged buttons to cover KeyDetector(28697939). Expect only Display2 to update; Display remains unaffected.
Enter ButtonBack (fileID 951466313, tag KeypadButton2). If Display2 is empty, expect no removal; KeyFeedback.keyHit set true for feedback.
Press Button(1) (fileID 195114625). Expect Display2 += '1' (len=1); KeyFeedback.keyHit=true on the button.
Press Button(2) (fileID 1362274088). Expect Display2: '12'; KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Press Button(3) (fileID 294570348). Expect Display2: '123'; KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Press Button(4) (fileID 691591083). Expect Display2: '1234' (max length reached at 4); KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Overflow guard: press Button(5) (fileID 851985417). Expect no change to Display2 (still '1234'); KeyFeedback.keyHit=true toggles feedback only.
Backspace: press ButtonBack (fileID 951466313). Expect Display2 becomes '123'; KeyFeedback.keyHit=true on ButtonBack.
Append after backspace: press Button(6) (fileID 2087223067). Expect Display2: '1236' (back to max length); KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Attempt another overflow: press Button(7) (fileID 2122760608). Expect Display2 unchanged (still 4 chars); KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Confirm path: press ButtoConfirm (fileID 1619357597). With Display2 length > 0, expect KeyDetector(28697939) to call KeyPadControll.CheckIfCorrect(int). Display2 changes to 'Correct' or 'Retry'.

Non-numeric reset: press Button(8) (fileID 1249007308). If previous Confirm set non-numeric text (e.g., 'Retry'/'Correct'), expect KeyDetector to clear Display2 then append '8'. KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Press Button(9) (fileID 571652021). Expect Display2 append '9' (e.g., becomes '89'); KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.
Press Button(0) (fileID 354067704). Expect Display2 append '0' (e.g., '890'); KeyFeedback.keyHit=true.

Final confirm: press ButtoConfirm (fileID 1619357597) again. Expect KeyPadControll.CheckIfCorrect invoked with current numeric Display2; Display2 updates to 'Correct' or 'Retry'. This completes coverage for: append, cap-at-4, backspace, non-numeric reset, and confirm.

Validate TeleportController wiring and activate teleport mode. MonoBehaviour id 900660956 on RightHand uses script guid b095db2c7e1fcd24ea55dcbfd4c44ef4 (TeleportController source provided). Serialized fields: baseControllerGameObject -> fileID 452756132, teleportationGameObject -> fileID 41270343, teleport=false, teleportActivationReference bound to input action (guid c348712bda248c246b8c49b3db54643f). onTeleportActivate.m_PersistentCalls includes toggling XRBaseController enableInputActions and Behaviour enabled states, GameObject.SetActive, XRBaseController.hideControllerModel, and Sphere.swapTeleportMode (script fileID 33018430). Simulate a performed input on teleportActivationReference to drive TeleportToggle -> TeleportModeActivate -> onTeleportActivate.

Toggle teleport off to cover cancel path. Same TeleportController (MonoBehaviour id 900660956, guid b095db2c7e1fcd24ea55dcbfd4c44ef4). Simulate a second performed input on teleportActivationReference to invoke TeleportToggle -> TeleportModeCancel -> onTeleportCancel (no persistent calls serialized). Expect no additional UnityEvent calls beyond cancel.

Manually invoke grip activate UnityEvent despite component being disabled, to ensure event coverage. MonoBehaviour id 900660957 uses script guid 474205c7e80edfc4384b329a9ec35a65 (gripActivate source provided) and is m_Enabled=0, so Start() will not bind input callbacks; we therefore trigger its serialized UnityEvents directly. onGripActivate.m_PersistentCalls -> Sphere.disableSphere (script fileID 33018430).

Manually invoke grip cancel UnityEvent to complete coverage. Same gripActivate (MonoBehaviour id 900660957, guid 474205c7e80edfc4384b329a9ec35a65). onGripCancel.m_PersistentCalls -> Sphere.enableSphere (script fileID 33018430).

Activate teleport mode via TeleportController. MonoBehaviour id 79813987 on LeftHand uses script guid b095db2c7e1fcd24ea55dcbfd4c44ef4 (TeleportController source provided). Serialized fields: baseControllerGameObject -> fileID 1887085101, teleportationGameObject -> fileID 806232320, teleport=false, teleportActivationReference bound to input action (fileID -6131295136447488360, guid c348712bda248c246b8c49b3db54643f). onTeleportActivate has persistent calls to XRBaseController.set_enableInputActions, Behaviour.set_enabled, GameObject.SetActive, XRBaseController.set_hideControllerModel, etc. Simulate a performed input on teleportActivationReference to drive Start->TeleportToggle(false)->TeleportModeActivate->onTeleportActivate.

Cancel teleport mode via TeleportController to cover the else-path. Same MonoBehaviour id 79813987 (guid b095db2c7e1fcd24ea55dcbfd4c44ef4). After activation, simulate a second performed input on teleportActivationReference to invoke TeleportToggle(true)->TeleportModeCancel->onTeleportCancel.

Manually invoke grip activate UnityEvent to ensure coverage, since gripActivate (MonoBehaviour id 79813988, guid 474205c7e80edfc4384b329a9ec35a65) is m_Enabled=0 and will not bind input callbacks. onGripActivate.m_PersistentCalls -> Sphere.disableSphere (target script fileID 28697940).

Manually invoke grip cancel UnityEvent to complete coverage of gripActivate. Same MonoBehaviour id 79813988 (guid 474205c7e80edfc4384b329a9ec35a65). onGripCancel.m_PersistentCalls -> Sphere.enableSphere (target script fileID 28697940).

Simulate XRDirectInteractor SelectEntered to execute serialized event calls. MonoBehaviour id 1887085104 uses script guid 4253f32900bcc4d499d675566142ded0 (XR Interaction Toolkit XRDirectInteractor). Scene meta config: m_SelectEntered.m_PersistentCalls -> Renderer (fileID 314576055).set_enabled(false). SphereCollider id 1887085103 isTrigger=1 allows Trigger interaction.

Simulate XRDirectInteractor SelectExited to execute serialized event calls. Same MonoBehaviour id 1887085104 (guid 4253f32900bcc4d499d675566142ded0). Scene meta config: m_SelectExited.m_PersistentCalls -> Renderer (fileID 314576055).set_enabled(true).

Exercise BodyCollider position-follow logic while ensuring collider-based interaction eligibility. MonoBehaviour id 822151145 (script guid 349b1e6dd63d78147bcda3eab8cc058e) matches the provided BodyCollider script and is configured with MainCamera=fileID 1241724591 and feet=fileID 1054322465. A CapsuleCollider is present (id 822151146, m_IsTrigger=0), making Trigger action valid. During this trigger window, the script’s Update should set BodyCollider.position = (MainCamera.x, feet.y, MainCamera.z). To achieve coverage of Update with non-trivial changes, the MainCamera’s x/z and feet’s y should change at least once within the window.
